name: Mutation Testing
def: Measures test-suite quality by introducing small code faults and checking tests catch them.
problem: Expose weak tests and false confidence that line coverage alone hides.
mechanism: Tools mutate the code (flip operators, drop statements); a mutant "survives" if no test fails, revealing gaps in assertions.
benefits: Reveals tests that don’t actually assert behaviour
failures: Treating coverage as quality without this check
whenNot: Very large suites without incremental tooling
myths: "High coverage = good tests" — mutation testing routinely disproves this
